coin_profile.name: YLDS
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: YLDS (YLDS) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #101 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: YLDS exhibits minimal protocol moat despite perfect dilution control. As a cash-flow protocol at rank 101, it lacks network effects (network_demand: 4), has weak competitive defensibility with no apparent technical barriers to forking, and shows poor holder value accrual (score: 5) indicating token utility doesn't meaningfully capture protocol economics. The extremely low protocol_maturity (2) and mediocre capital efficiency (5) suggest YLDS operates in a crowded, easily replicable market segment with limited ecosystem lock-in or switching costs for users and capital.

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.7/10 and survivability 56.7%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -29.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.7/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-29.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 56.7% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

bull_points: <cite index="2-2">YLDS launched on Stellar on May 5, 2026, the most regulated-RWA-dense network in crypto</cite>, signaling institutional-grade ecosystem expansion that could attract enterprise integrations and drive near-term volume growth.

<cite index="3-10">Trading volume of YLDS surged 125% in a single day to $10.93M</cite>, indicating renewed market interest and positioning for potential near-term momentum as awareness of the Stellar deployment spreads.

<cite index="7-18,7-20">The GENIUS Act established consistent federal standards for stablecoins in July 2025 and enterprise integration has picked up pace</cite>, favoring SEC-registered yield-bearing products like YLDS over unregulated alternatives in institutional procurement cycles.
bear_points: <cite index="3-1">YLDS has seen minimal price movement with only a 0.00% change over the past 7 days despite showing marginally better performance than broader stablecoin markets</cite>, suggesting limited near-term catalyst momentum despite multi-network expansion.

<cite index="2-4">YLDS expanded across three networks in six months (Provenance in Feb 2025, Solana in Nov 2025, Stellar in May 2026)</cite>, yet flat price action suggests network expansion alone is insufficient to drive short-term appreciation.

At a $509M market cap with full circulating supply already in circulation and 100% of max supply deployed, YLDS lacks a near-term dilution story or scarcity-driven catalyst that typically initiates 3-6 month rallies in crypto markets.

bull_points: <cite index="2-1,2-22">YLDS is the first SEC-registered yield-bearing dollar product deployed on Stellar, which is the most regulated-RWA-dense network in crypto</cite>, establishing a foundational moat in institutional stablecoin infrastructure that compounds over 1-5 years.

<cite index="7-28,7-29">Corporates are treating tokenized dollars as 24/7 liquid cash and in 2026 on-chain dollars are expected to graduate from pilots into enterprise plumbing across treasury workflows, cross-border settlement and programmable B2B payments</cite>, positioning YLDS as a core rails asset.

With 100% of maximum supply already in circulation and an FDV-to-MC ratio of 1.0, dilution risk is completely eliminated for long-term holders, enabling pure value accrual if adoption and transaction volume scale as projected in stablecoin enterprise use cases.
bear_points: The protocol lacks any disclosed multi-year fee revenue data or annual_fees_usd metrics, leaving long-term token economics and holder value accrual structurally unproven despite its position as a cash-flow protocol over a 3-5 year horizon.

YLDS competes in an increasingly crowded regulated stablecoin market where established players like USDC and emerging competitors benefit from larger network effects; without demonstrated fee-capture mechanisms or unique protocol revenue streams, differentiation risk remains material.

Regulatory disruption from future GENIUS Act amendments, state-level restrictions on yield products, or OCC supervisory tightening could materially constrain YLDS adoption growth and limit the economic moat expected to develop over a 1-5 year period.

metric_assessments.fee_growth: The score of 4 reflects uncertainty around YLDS's ability to grow fees as the protocol scales, with no actual annual fee data available to validate revenue trends. The protocol appears to be generating some fee activity relative to its market cap (2.37% volume-to-MC ratio suggests reasonable transaction flow), but lacks the transparency or scale to demonstrate consistent fee expansion. Without published fee metrics or historical growth patterns, investors cannot assess whether YLDS is capturing more value from users over time or if fee generation is stagnant.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: A perfect 5 reflects YLDS's positioning as a top-tier protocol by market cap rank within its peer set, suggesting the market has allocated capital to this project relative to others in the cash-flow category. However, this ranking-based score doesn't measure whether that capital is actually being deployed to generate sustainable returns—it only confirms relative market recognition. The lack of PE or PS ratios means there's no way to verify whether YLDS converts that allocated capital into measurable profits or fees.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: The maximum score of 5 appears to assume the protocol's mechanism successfully distributes fees or cash flows back to token holders, but this assessment is labeled 'estimated' and cannot be independently verified without actual fee or dividend data. If YLDS operates a genuine cash-flow model where holders receive a share of protocol revenues, this score is justified; if the mechanism is theoretical or poorly executed, the score overstates reality. The absence of supporting financial data (annual fees, distribution history) makes this the most speculative assessment in the profile.
metric_assessments.network_demand: A score of 4 corresponds to 2.37% daily volume relative to market cap, which indicates modest but not exceptional trading or usage activity for a $509M protocol. This level of volume-to-market-cap ratio suggests YLDS has a functional user base but isn't experiencing extraordinary demand surges or network effects that would drive higher velocity. The metric places YLDS in the middle range—active enough to be real, but not explosive enough to signal runaway adoption.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: The perfect score of 10 reflects that 100% of YLDS's token supply is in circulation, meaning there is zero token dilution risk from future emissions or vesting schedules. This is a genuine strength: holders face no hidden supply overhang that could pressure the price later. However, this only matters if the protocol was designed with a fixed supply from inception; it doesn't guarantee the tokenomics are sound or that past dilution didn't already damage early holders.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: The very low score of 2 directly reflects YLDS's rank of 101 in market cap—a position that signals either early-stage development, limited adoption, or failure to achieve the scale of top protocols. Being outside the top 100 suggests YLDS has not yet proven sustainable product-market fit or network effects at enterprise scale. This immaturity limits visibility into how the protocol will perform during market stress or competition, making it a speculative bet rather than a battle-tested system.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: YLDS carries a Dangerous rating because its overall quality score of 4.7/10 (47th percentile) is dragged down by critically low protocol maturity (score: 2) and unverifiable value accrual mechanisms (no actual fee or earnings data). While dilution control is pristine and network demand is moderate, the protocol lacks the financial transparency, historical track record, or market traction to justify confidence in its cash-flow thesis.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: YLDS is a cash-flow protocol attempting to distribute fees or revenues to token holders, positioned outside the top 100 projects with a $509M market cap and a price hovering near $1.00. The core strength lies in its zero token dilution—all supply is already circulating—which eliminates a major source of future pressure. The fundamental risk is that the protocol's value-accrual mechanism (holder payouts, fee capture) remains unproven, with no published annual fees, earnings ratios, or distribution history to validate whether the cash-flow model actually works in practice. The protocol ranks at #101, suggesting it has not yet built the network effects or user adoption needed to compete with established layer-1s or specialized DeFi platforms, making it a high-risk bet on an immature system. Beginners should avoid YLDS unless they fully understand the specific cash-flow mechanics and can afford to lose their entire investment.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: YLDS scores 4.7/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.
